shortDescription.text: Detects general malicious software characteristics and behaviors.

fullDescription.text: This rule identifies code or instructions exhibiting characteristics commonly associated with malware, such as unauthorized system access, data exfiltration, or system compromise.

shortDescription.text: Identifies attempts to exfiltrate sensitive data.

fullDescription.text: This rule flags behaviors indicative of data exfiltration, including sending data to unauthorized external servers, accessing sensitive files, or bypassing security controls to transmit information.

shortDescription.text: Detects potential remote code execution vulnerabilities or attempts.

fullDescription.text: This rule identifies patterns that could lead to or are part of a remote code execution attack, allowing an attacker to run arbitrary code on the target system.

shortDescription.text: Flags mechanisms used for maintaining persistence on a system.

fullDescription.text: This rule detects techniques used by malicious software to ensure continued execution across reboots or after system compromise, such as modifying startup entries or scheduling tasks.

shortDescription.text: Identifies insecure coding or configuration practices.

fullDescription.text: This rule highlights practices that, while not directly malicious, introduce security risks, such as hardcoding credentials, insecure secret storage, or bypassing secure authentication.

message.text: Analysis completed. No confirmed malicious intent or security issues found. The content describes standard and legitimate development practices for Expo Router UI.
